fields.summary: [Umbrella] Store, manage and serve per-framework application-timeline data

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description:
Now TimelineWebServices contains non-trivial logic to process the HTTP requests, manipulate the data, check the access, and interact with the timeline store.

I propose the move the data-oriented logic to a middle layer (so called TimelineDataManager), and TimelineWebServices only processes the requests, and call TimelineDataManager to complete the remaining tasks.

By doing this, we make the generic history module reuse TimelineDataManager internally (YARN-2033), invoking the putting/getting methods directly. Otherwise, we have to send the HTTP requests to TimelineWebServices to query the generic history data, which is not an efficient way.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Merging generic-history into the Timeline Store

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

Thanks for the input, Junping! Patch updated again.

bq. Shall we rename this method to putEntities?

This is a good question. AFAIK, PUT is used to update the entire resource and supposed to be idempotent. Therefore, I'm not sure it's an idea to change the method to PUT because once the entity is created, the following updates are actually appending more data to the existing one. The best fit should be PATCH, however, it requires the additional implementation at the web services side. Hence, somebody online suggested using POST for partial non-idempotent update as well. Anyway, i think it's not a simple code refactoring, I'll file another ticket to track the investigation of proper HTTP method for postEntities.

bq. In addition, I think we should have javadoc for public methods in TimelineDataManager.java. 

As TimelineDataManager is not the user-oriented interface, I added some simple javadoc for the public methods, and link them to TimelineStoreReader for argument details.
